# Cryptography Module - Full Documentation

# Cryptography Module - Additional Configuration

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

After you complete basic Cryptography Module configuration, you can perform additional configuration
tasks.

## Configure Use Random Initialization Vectors

Configure the **Use random IVs** field to use random initialization vectors (IVs). If you configure
this field, the decryption algorithm expects the IVs to be added to the beginning of the ciphertext
during decryption.

Follow these steps for the configuration:

1.  In Anypoint Studio, drag a Cryptography Module operation to your flow, for example **Jce sign**.
2.  Select the operation from the flow.
3.  In the operation configuration screen, click the plus sign to access the module global
    configuration.
4.  In the **Global Element Properties** window, enable the **Use random IVs** field.

![A configuration setting for Crypto JCE with options for keystore and random IV usage](_images/crypto-random-iv.png)

In the **XML editor** window, the configuration looks like this:

```xml
crypto:jce-config name="Crypto_Jce" doc:name="Crypto Jce" keystore="/Users/MuleSoft/Desktop/jcekeystore.jks" password="mulesoft" useRandomIVs="true">
		<crypto:jce-key-infos >
			<crypto:jce-symmetric-key-info keyId="key100" alias="jceclient100" password="mulesoft" />
		</crypto:jce-key-infos>
	</crypto:jce-config>
```

## See Also

- [Cryptography Module Examples](cryptography-module-examples)
- [Cryptography Module Reference 2.1](cryptography-module-reference)
---

# Cryptography Module Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Cryptography Module examples help you use JCE (PBE and KBE), PGP, and XML operations.

- [Configuring JCE Cryptography](cryptography-module-jce)  
  Configure the JCE (PBE and KBE) encryption operations.
- [Configuring PGP Cryptography](cryptography-module-pgp)  
  Configure the PGP encryption operations.
- [Configuring XML Cryptography](cryptography-module-xml)  
  Configure the XML encryption operations.
- [Cryptography Module Additional Configuration](cryptography-module-additional-configuration)  
  Configure the **Use random IVs** field to use random initialization vectors (IVs).

## See Also

- [Cryptography Module Reference 2.1](cryptography-module-reference)
- [Salesforce Help Center](https://help.salesforce.com/s/?language=en_US)
---

# Configuring JCE Cryptography

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The Java Cryptography Extension (JCE) strategy lets you use Javaâs powerful encryption features. You
have the flexibility to encrypt an entire message or just specific parts of it using either of these
two methods:

- Password-based encryption (PBE)  
  This method enables you to encrypt and sign content by simply providing an encryption password.
- Key-based encryption (KBE)  
  Similar to PGP and XML encryption, this method enables you to configure a symmetric or asymmetric
  key to perform encryption and signing operations.

Use the Cryptography Module JCE operations to configure these methods.

> [!NOTE] If youâre upgrading to Cryptography Module 2.1, configure the required Java dependencies
> so your project compiles. For details, refer to
> [Upgrading and Migrating Cryptography Module 2.x](cryptography-module-upgrade).

## Before You Begin

You are familiar with PBE and KBE encryption, concepts of public and private keys, and asymmetric
cryptography.

## Configure PBE Encryption

This method takes your password and runs it through a hash function to create a symmetric encryption
key. This generated key works with standard encryption algorithms. Because PBE only needs a
password, you donât need a separate Global Configuration Element for JCE PBE operations.

### Configure JCE PBE Operations in Anypoint Studio

Follow these steps:

1.  In Anypoint Studio, drag a **Cryptography Module** JCE PBE operation to the canvas flow.

    ![A palette menu for adding password-based encryption modules in a cryptographic flow](_images/crypto-jce-password.png)

2.  In the operation configuration window, set the **Algorithm**, **Password** **Password salt** and
    **Iteration count** properties: +git s image::crypto-jce-setting.png\["Configuration settings
    for JCE encrypt PBE with algorithm, password, password salt, iteration options"\]

### XML Examples

These are XML examples for each of the JCE PBE operations:

- **Jce encryption pbe**

  ```xml
  <crypto:jce-encrypt-pbe password="a-Sup3r_Secure-Passw0rd"/>
  ```

  If you donât specify an algorithm, the module uses `PBEWithHmacSHA256AndAES_128` .

- **Jce decrypt pbe**

  ```xml
  <crypto:jce-decrypt-pbe algorithm="PBEWithHmacSHA256AndAES_128" password="a-Sup3r_Secure-Passw0rd"/>
  ```

- **Jce sign pbe**

  ```xml
  <crypto:jce-sign-pbe password="a-Sup3r_Secure-Passw0rd"/>
  ```

  If you donât specify an algorithm, the module uses `PBEWithHmacSHA256`.

- **Jce validate pbe**

  ```xml
  <crypto:jce-validate-pbe password="a-Sup3r_Secure-Passw0rd" algorithm="PBEWithHmacSHA256" expected="#[vars.expectedSignature]"/>
  ```

  The `expected` parameter defines the signature used to validate the message.

## Configure KBE Encryption

Configure a symmetric or asymmetric key to perform encryption and signing operations.

### Configure JCE KBE Operations in Anypoint Studio

Follow these steps:

1.  In Anypoint Studio, drag a Cryptography Module JCE KBE operation to the canvas flow.

    ![A crypto-examples project with components for encryption and decryption](_images/mruntime-crypto-jce-add.png)

2.  In the operation configuration window, select an existing **Module configuration**, or create a
    new one by configuring the **Keystore**, **Type** (JKS, JCEKS, PKCS12), and **Password**
    parameters.

    You can also add symmetric or asymmetric key information to use in sign operations:

    ![Global configuration properties for Crypto JCE with keystore details](_images/mruntime-crypto-jce-global-config.png)

3.  Configure **Key selection** by using a **Key id** value previously defined in the module
    configuration, or define a new one for this operation:

    ![A configuration panel for JCE encryption showing settings for content, algorithm, and key selection](_images/mruntime-crypto-jce-config.png)

4.  Select the algorithm to use during the operation.

### XML Examples

These XML examples show a JCE configuration that defines symmetric and asymmetric keys and different
operations using these keys.

- Configuration

  In this example, the JCE configuration contains a keystore with different types of keys:

  ```xml
  <crypto:jce-config name="jceConfig" keystore="jce/keys.jceks" password="123456" type="JCEKS">
      <crypto:jce-key-infos>
          <crypto:jce-symmetric-key-info keyId="aes128" alias="aes128" password="123456"/>
          <crypto:jce-symmetric-key-info keyId="blowfish" alias="blowfish" password="123456"/>
          <crypto:jce-symmetric-key-info keyId="hmacsha256" alias="hmacsha256" password="123456"/>
          <crypto:jce-asymmetric-key-info keyId="rsa" alias="myrsakey" password="123456"/>
          <crypto:jce-asymmetric-key-info keyId="dsa" alias="mydsakey" password="123456"/>
      </crypto:jce-key-infos>
  </crypto:jce-config>
  ```

- Asymmetric Encryption

  In this example, the operations **Jce encrypt** and **Jce decrypt** use the asymmetric keys
  defined in the previous configuration.

  Encrypting a Message

  ```xml
  <crypto:jce-encrypt config-ref="jceConfig" keyId="rsa" algorithm="RSA"/>
  ```

  Decrypting a Message

  ```xml
  <crypto:jce-decrypt config-ref="jceConfig" keyId="rsa" algorithm="RSA"/>
  ```

- Symmetric Encryption

  In this example, the operations **Jce encrypt** and **Jce decrypt** use the symmetric keys defined
  in the previous configuration.

  Encrypting a Message

  ```xml
  <crypto:jce-encrypt config-ref="jceConfig" keyId="aes128" algorithm="AES"/>
  ```

  Decrypting a Message

  ```xml
  <crypto:jce-decrypt config-ref="jceConfig" keyId="aes128" algorithm="AES"/>
  ```

- Signature and Validation

  In this example, the **Jce sign** and **Jce validate** operations use a key defined in the
  previous configuration:

  Signing a Message

  ```xml
  <crypto:jce-sign config-ref="jceConfig" keyId="dsa" algorithm="SHA256withDSA"/>
  ```

  Validating a Signature

  ```xml
  <crypto:jce-validate config-ref="jceConfig" keyId="dsa" algorithm="SHA256withDSA" expected="#[vars.expectedSignature]"/>
  ```

  The `expected` parameter defines the signature used to validate the message.

## See Also

- [Cryptography Module Examples](cryptography-module-examples)
- [Cryptography Module Reference 2.1](cryptography-module-reference)
---

# Configuring PGP Cryptography

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Mule runtime engine (Mule) can encrypt entire messages or just parts of them using Pretty Good
Privacy (PGP). PGP secures messages by combining data compression and encryption. The compression
helps reduce the message size, which can speed up transmission within your Mule app. PGP encryption
is more resource-intensive than JCE or XML encryption due to its increased complexity.

There are two main PGP scenarios:

- Encryption  
  Encrypt an outgoing message in your Mule app using another partyâs public key.
- Decryption  
  Decrypt an incoming message in your Mule app using your own private key.

Use the Cryptography Module PGP operations to configure these scenarios.

> [!NOTE] If youâre upgrading to Cryptography Module 2.1, configure the required Java dependencies
> so your project compiles. For details, refer to
> [Upgrading and Migrating Cryptography Module 2.x](cryptography-module-upgrade).

> [!NOTE] This guide covers the main PGP operations and common use cases. For a complete list of all
> available parameters and their default values, refer to
> [Cryptography Module Reference 2.1](cryptography-module-reference).

## Before You Begin

You are familiar with PGP encryption, concepts of public and private keys, and asymmetric
cryptography.

## FIPS Compliance Considerations

> [!IMPORTANT] PGP Encrypt operations are not supported in FIPS environments, including MuleSoft
> Government Cloud.
>
> The OpenPGP standard (RFC 4880) mandates the use of RSAES-PKCS1-v1_5 for encrypting the session
> key, which is blocked in FIPS approved mode. This limitation exists regardless of the symmetric
> encryption algorithm chosen.
>
> Supported PGP operations in FIPS:
>
> - PGP Decrypt (for legacy data)
> - PGP Sign
> - PGP Validate
>
> For complete FIPS migration details, refer to
> [cryptography-module-upgrade.adoc#scope-and-limitations-fips](cryptography-module-upgrade#scope-and-limitations-fips).

## Configure PGP Operations in Anypoint Studio

Follow these steps:

1.  In Anypoint Studio, drag a Cryptography Module PGP operation to the canvas flow.

    ![A palette menu for adding PGP encryption and decryption modules](_images/mruntime-crypto-pgp-add.png)

2.  In the operation configuration window, select an existing **Module configuration**, or create a
    new one by configuring the **Public keyring** and **Private keyring** files.

    You can also add asymmetric key information to be used in the sign operations:

    ![Global element properties for Crypto PGP with keyring details](_images/mruntime-crypto-pgp-global-config.png)

3.  Configure **Key selection** by using a **Key id** value previously defined in the module
    configuration, or define a new one for this operation:

    ![A configuration settings for PGP encrypt with algorithm and key selection](_images/mruntime-crypto-pgp-config.png)

4.  Select the algorithm to use during the operation.

## Work with Subkeys

According to the RFC-4880 standard, a PGP key can contain multiple subkeys. When youâre working with
these subkeys, use the fingerprint attribute within the `<crypto:pgp-asymmetric-key-info>` XML
element to specify which subkey you want to use.

For instance, if you have separate subkeys for signing and encryption, and they use different
algorithms, for example, DSA for signing and ElGamal for encryption, reference the correct subkeyâs
fingerprint for each operation. Therefore, use the ElGamal fingerprint to encrypt your messages and
the DSA fingerprint to sign them.

## Encrypt Messages with Public Keys

When encrypting messages with PGP, you (the sender) must encrypt the content using the receiverâs
public key. Therefore, to encrypt messages in your Mule app using someone elseâs public key, add
their public key to your keyring. When you configure a new PGP operation in your Mule app, provide
your keyring file. The encryption module then uses this file to retrieve the necessary public key
for encryption.

Follow these steps:

1.  Use a tool like GPG Suite to import the other partyâs public key.
2.  With the same tool, export the public key, making sure to select binary as the output format.
    This creates a keyring file with a `.gpg` extension.
3.  Place the `.gpg` keyring file in a location where your Mule app can access it during runtime.

Example: PGP Configuration

```xml
<crypto:pgp-config name="encrypt-conf" publicKeyring="pgp/pubring.gpg">
    <crypto:pgp-key-infos>
        <crypto:pgp-asymmetric-key-info keyId="myself" fingerprint="DE3F10F1B6B7F221"/>
    </crypto:pgp-key-infos>
</crypto:pgp-config>
```

- Use the **Pgp encrypt** operation to returns an ASCII-armored encrypted payload, which is suitable
  for sending over plain-text channels:

  ```xml
  <crypto:pgp-encrypt config-ref="encrypt-conf" keyId="myself"/>
  ```

  Use the `disableMDC` parameter to disable modification detection code if needed for compatibility
  with legacy systems. For all available parameters, refer to
  [cryptography-module-reference.adoc#PgpEncrypt](cryptography-module-reference#PgpEncrypt).

- Use the **Pgp encrypt binary** operation to return a binary output instead:

  ```xml
  <crypto:pgp-encrypt-binary config-ref="pgp-conf" keyId="recipient"/>
  ```

  Producing a binary output is faster than using ASCII-armored. However, the output is not standard
  and might not be ideal to send to other systems for decryption.

  > [!NOTE] The `disableMDC` parameter is also available for this operation. For all available
  > parameters, refer to
  > [cryptography-module-reference.adoc#PgpEncryptBinary](cryptography-module-reference#PgpEncryptBinary).

- Use the **Pgp binary to armored** operation to send a payload with binary output to another
  system, and transform it to ASCII-armored:

  ```xml
  <crypto:pgp-binary-to-armored/>
  ```

  This operation has a single input parameter, the message payload to transform.

## Encrypt and Sign

Besides just encrypting, you can also encrypt and sign a message in one atomic operation. This
returns a message in ASCII-armored format, similar to a purely encrypted one, but with an added
signature inside the encrypted content. This signature acts as an integrity check, verifying the
original message hasnât been tampered with.

To encrypt and sign, the private key of the signer (typically the sender) must be present in the
private keyring. This process always produces an ASCII-armored output.

Using the **Pgp encrypt and sign** operation:

```xml
<crypto:pgp-encrypt-and-sign config-ref="encrypt-conf">
    <crypto:encryption-key-selection keyId="recipient-key-id" />
    <crypto:sign-key-selection keyId="signer-key-id" />
</crypto:pgp-encrypt-and-sign>
```

## Decrypt

For PGP decryption, the message receiver must use their private key to decrypt content that was
encrypted with a public key. This means the receiver needs to share their public key with anyone who
will be sending them encrypted messages.

Example: PGP Configuration

```xml
<crypto:pgp-config name="decrypt-conf" privateKeyring="pgp/secring.gpg">
    <crypto:pgp-key-infos>
        <crypto:pgp-asymmetric-key-info keyId="myself" fingerprint="DE3F10F1B6B7F221" passphrase="mule1234"/>
    </crypto:pgp-key-infos>
</crypto:pgp-config>
```

In the previous example, notice that you must provide at least three parameters to be able to use
the private key ring in the decrypt operation:

- Key ID (`keyId`): the internal ID that allows you to reference this key from an operation.
- Key Fingerprint (`fingerprint`): The last 16 characters of your key fingerprint, which can be
  obtained from your external GPG tool, such as GPG Keychain.
- Passphrase (`passphrase`): The passphrase of the private key.

Using the **Pgp decrypt** operation

```xml
<crypto:pgp-decrypt config-ref="decrypt-conf"/>
```

> [!NOTE] Use the `validateIfSignatureFound` parameter to automatically validate embedded signatures
> during decryption. For all available parameters, refer to
> [cryptography-module-reference.adoc#PgpDecrypt](cryptography-module-reference#PgpDecrypt).

## Sign

Sign a message using a configured private key.

Example: PGP Configuration

```xml
<crypto:pgp-config name="sign-conf" privateKeyring="pgp/secring.gpg">
    <crypto:pgp-key-infos>
        <crypto:pgp-asymmetric-key-info keyId="myself" fingerprint="DE3F10F1B6B7F221" passphrase="mule1234"/>
    </crypto:pgp-key-infos>
</crypto:pgp-config>
```

Using the **Pgp sign** operation:

```xml
<crypto:pgp-sign config-ref="sign-conf" keyId="myself"/>
```

## Validate a Signature

Validate the signature of a message using the signerâs public key.

Example: PGP Configuration

```xml
<crypto:pgp-config name="validate-conf" publicKeyring="pgp/pubring.gpg">
    <crypto:pgp-key-infos>
        <crypto:pgp-asymmetric-key-info keyId="signer" fingerprint="DE3F10F1B6B7F221"/>
    </crypto:pgp-key-infos>
</crypto:pgp-config>
```

Using the **Pgp validate** operation:

```xml
<crypto:pgp-validate config-ref="validate-conf" value="#[payload]" expected="#[vars.expected]"/>
```

## See Also

- [Cryptography Module Examples](cryptography-module-examples)
- [Cryptography Module Reference 2.1](cryptography-module-reference)
---

# Cryptography Module Reference 2.1

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Cryptography Module provides cryptography capabilities to a Mule application.

## Configurations

---

### Jce

JCE configuration for Java keystores and inline keys.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Keystore</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path to the keystore file.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JKS</p></li><li><p>JCEKS</p></li><li><p>PKCS12</p></li><li><p>BCFKS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Type of the keystore.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password for unlocking the keystore.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jce Key Infos</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#JceAsymmetricKeyInfo">Jce Asymmetric Key Info</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#JceSymmetricKeyInfo">Jce Symmetric Key Info</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>List of keys to be considered, with internal IDs for referencing them.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use random IVs</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Use random initial vectors (IVs) for CBC algorithms. In case of decryption, it assumes IV is prepended on the ciphertext.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID used to reference this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Supported Operations

- [Jce Decrypt](#JceDecrypt)
- [Jce Encrypt](#JceEncrypt)
- [Jce Sign](#JceSign)
- [Jce Validate](#JceValidate)
- [Pgp Decrypt](#PgpDecrypt)
- [Pgp Encrypt](#PgpEncrypt)
- [Pgp Encrypt And Sign](#PgpEncryptAndSign)
- [Pgp Encrypt Binary](#PgpEncryptBinary)
- [Pgp Sign](#PgpSign)
- [Pgp Sign Binary](#PgpSignBinary)
- [Pgp Validate](#PgpValidate)
- [Xml Decrypt](#XmlDecrypt)
- [Xml Encrypt](#XmlEncrypt)
- [Xml Sign](#XmlSign)
- [Xml Validate](#XmlValidate)
- [Calculate Checksum](#CalculateChecksum)
- [Validate Checksum](#ValidateChecksum)

---

### Pgp

Keystore configuration for GPG. Contains a list of keys with internal names to use in the
operations.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Public Keyring</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Public key ring file.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Private Keyring</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Private key ring file.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pgp Key Infos</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#PgpAsymmetricKeyInfo">Pgp Asymmetric Key Info</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>List of keys to be considered, with internal IDs for referencing them.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID used to reference this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Operations

## Jce Decrypt

`<crypto:jce-decrypt>`

Decrypt a stream using JCE, with a key.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to decrypt</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cipher</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A raw cipher string in the form "algorithm/mode/padding" according to the Java crypto documentation, for example <code>AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding</code>. Note that GCM mode is currently not supported, and not all algorithm/mode/padding combinations are valid.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AES</p></li><li><p>AESWrap</p></li><li><p>ARCFOUR</p></li><li><p>Blowfish</p></li><li><p>DES</p></li><li><p>DESede</p></li><li><p>RC2</p></li><li><p>DESedeWrap</p></li><li><p>RSA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Algorithm from a list of valid definitions, this is preferred.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The key ID, as defined in the JCE configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jce Key Info</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#JceAsymmetricKeyInfo">Jce Asymmetric Key Info</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#JceSymmetricKeyInfo">Jce Symmetric Key Info</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>An inline key definition.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that storesoperationâs output will be placed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Jce](#Jce)

### Throws

- CRYPTO:DECRYPTION
- CRYPTO:ENCRYPTION
- CRYPTO:KEY
- CRYPTO:MISSING_KEY
- CRYPTO:PARAMETERS
- CRYPTO:PASSPHRASE

## Jce Encrypt

`<crypto:jce-encrypt>`

Encrypt a stream using JCE, with a key.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to encrypt</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cipher</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A raw cipher string in the form "algorithm/mode/padding" according to the Java crypto documentation, for example <code>AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding</code>. Note that GCM mode is currently not supported, and not all algorithm/mode/padding combinations are valid.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AES</p></li><li><p>AESWrap</p></li><li><p>ARCFOUR</p></li><li><p>Blowfish</p></li><li><p>DES</p></li><li><p>DESede</p></li><li><p>RC2</p></li><li><p>DESedeWrap</p></li><li><p>RSA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Algorithm from a list of valid definitions, this is preferred.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The key ID, as defined in the JCE configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jce Key Info</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#JceAsymmetricKeyInfo">Jce Asymmetric Key Info</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#JceSymmetricKeyInfo">Jce Symmetric Key Info</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>An inline key definition.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that storesoperationâs output will be placed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Jce](#Jce)

### Throws

- CRYPTO:ENCRYPTION
- CRYPTO:KEY
- CRYPTO:MISSING_KEY
- CRYPTO:PARAMETERS

## Jce Sign

`<crypto:jce-sign>`

Sign a stream using JCE, with a password.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to sign</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>MD2withRSA</p></li><li><p>MD5withRSA</p></li><li><p>SHA1withRSA</p></li><li><p>SHA224withRSA</p></li><li><p>SHA256withRSA</p></li><li><p>SHA384withRSA</p></li><li><p>SHA512withRSA</p></li><li><p>NONEwithDSA</p></li><li><p>SHA1withDSA</p></li><li><p>SHA224withDSA</p></li><li><p>SHA256withDSA</p></li><li><p>HmacMD5</p></li><li><p>HmacSHA1</p></li><li><p>HmacSHA224</p></li><li><p>HmacSHA256</p></li><li><p>HmacSHA384</p></li><li><p>HmacSHA512</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>the algorithm used for signing</p></td><td><p>HmacSHA256</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The key ID, as defined in the JCE configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jce Key Info</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#JceAsymmetricKeyInfo">Jce Asymmetric Key Info</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#JceSymmetricKeyInfo">Jce Symmetric Key Info</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>An inline key definition.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that storesoperationâs output will be placed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Jce](#Jce)

### Throws

- CRYPTO:KEY
- CRYPTO:MISSING_KEY
- CRYPTO:PARAMETERS
- CRYPTO:PASSPHRASE
- CRYPTO:SIGNATURE

## Jce Validate

`<crypto:jce-validate>`

Validate a stream against a signature, using a password.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the message to authenticate</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expected</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>the signature to validate</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>MD2withRSA</p></li><li><p>MD5withRSA</p></li><li><p>SHA1withRSA</p></li><li><p>SHA224withRSA</p></li><li><p>SHA256withRSA</p></li><li><p>SHA384withRSA</p></li><li><p>SHA512withRSA</p></li><li><p>NONEwithDSA</p></li><li><p>SHA1withDSA</p></li><li><p>SHA224withDSA</p></li><li><p>SHA256withDSA</p></li><li><p>HmacMD5</p></li><li><p>HmacSHA1</p></li><li><p>HmacSHA224</p></li><li><p>HmacSHA256</p></li><li><p>HmacSHA384</p></li><li><p>HmacSHA512</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>the algorithm used for signing</p></td><td><p>HmacSHA256</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The key ID, as defined in the JCE configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jce Key Info</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#JceAsymmetricKeyInfo">Jce Asymmetric Key Info</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#JceSymmetricKeyInfo">Jce Symmetric Key Info</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>An inline key definition.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Jce](#Jce)

### Throws

- CRYPTO:MISSING_KEY
- CRYPTO:PARAMETERS
- CRYPTO:VALIDATION

## Xml Decrypt

`<crypto:xml-decrypt>`

Decrypt the XML document.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the document to decrypt</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Element Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The key ID, as defined in the JCE configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jce Key Info</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#JceAsymmetricKeyInfo">Jce Asymmetric Key Info</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#JceSymmetricKeyInfo">Jce Symmetric Key Info</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>An inline key definition.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that storesoperationâs output will be placed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Jce](#Jce)

### Throws

- CRYPTO:DECRYPTION
- CRYPTO:ENCRYPTION
- CRYPTO:KEY
- CRYPTO:MISSING_KEY
- CRYPTO:PARAMETERS
- CRYPTO:PASSPHRASE

## Xml Encrypt

`<crypto:xml-encrypt>`

Encrypt the XML document.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the document to encrypt</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AES_CBC</p></li><li><p>AES_GCM</p></li><li><p>TRIPLEDES</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>the algorithm for encryption</p></td><td><p>AES_CBC</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Element Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>the path to the element to encrypt, if empty the whole document is considered</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Encrypt Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>encrypt the XML element or its content, if false the whole XML element is encrypted.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The key ID, as defined in the JCE configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jce Key Info</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#JceAsymmetricKeyInfo">Jce Asymmetric Key Info</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#JceSymmetricKeyInfo">Jce Symmetric Key Info</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>An inline key definition.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Encryption Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>RSA15</p></li><li><p>RSA_OAEP</p></li><li><p>RSA_OAEP_11</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>RSA_OAEP</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Canonicalization algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>EXCLUSIVE</p></li><li><p>EXCLUSIVE_WITH_COMMENTS</p></li><li><p>INCLUSIVE</p></li><li><p>INCLUSE_WITH_COMMENTS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>EXCLUSIVE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Digest Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>RIPEMD160</p></li><li><p>SHA1</p></li><li><p>SHA256</p></li><li><p>SHA512</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>SHA512</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that storesoperationâs output will be placed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Jce](#Jce)

### Throws

- CRYPTO:ENCRYPTION
- CRYPTO:KEY
- CRYPTO:MISSING_KEY
- CRYPTO:PARAMETERS

## Xml Sign

`<crypto:xml-sign>`

Sign an XML document.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the XML document to sign</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Digest Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>RIPEMD160</p></li><li><p>SHA1</p></li><li><p>SHA256</p></li><li><p>SHA512</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>the hashing algorithm for signing</p></td><td><p>SHA256</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Canonicalization Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>EXCLUSIVE</p></li><li><p>EXCLUSIVE_WITH_COMMENTS</p></li><li><p>INCLUSIVE</p></li><li><p>INCLUSE_WITH_COMMENTS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>the canonicalization method for whitespace and namespace unification</p></td><td><p>EXCLUSIVE</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>DETACHED</p></li><li><p>ENVELOPED</p></li><li><p>ENVELOPING</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>the type of signature to create</p></td><td><p>ENVELOPED</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Element Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>for internally detached signatures, an unambiguous XPath expression resolving to the element to sign</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The key ID, as defined in the JCE configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jce Key Info</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#JceAsymmetricKeyInfo">Jce Asymmetric Key Info</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#JceSymmetricKeyInfo">Jce Symmetric Key Info</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>An inline key definition.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that storesoperationâs output will be placed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Jce](#Jce)

### Throws

- CRYPTO:KEY
- CRYPTO:MISSING_KEY
- CRYPTO:PARAMETERS
- CRYPTO:PASSPHRASE
- CRYPTO:SIGNATURE

## Xml Validate

`<crypto:xml-validate>`

Validate an XML signed document.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the document to verify (includes the signature)</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Element Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>for internally detached signatures, an unambiguous XPath expression resolving to the signed element</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Inline Certificate If Present</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Use inline defined certificate if found</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The key ID, as defined in the JCE configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jce Key Info</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#JceAsymmetricKeyInfo">Jce Asymmetric Key Info</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#JceSymmetricKeyInfo">Jce Symmetric Key Info</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>An inline key definition.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Jce](#Jce)

### Throws

- CRYPTO:MISSING_KEY
- CRYPTO:PARAMETERS
- CRYPTO:VALIDATION

## Pgp Decrypt

`<crypto:pgp-decrypt>`

Decrypt a stream using PGP, giving the original data as a result. The decryption is done with the
private key, so the secret passphrase must be provided.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to decrypt</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>the internal file name to decrypt, if not present the first will be used</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Validate If Signature Found</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If the message being decrypted contains a signature, choose whether or not to validate the decrypted message against it</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that storesoperationâs output will be placed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Pgp](#Pgp)

### Throws

- CRYPTO:DECRYPTION
- CRYPTO:ENCRYPTION
- CRYPTO:KEY
- CRYPTO:MISSING_KEY
- CRYPTO:PASSPHRASE

## Pgp Encrypt

`<crypto:pgp-encrypt>`

Encrypt a stream using PGP, giving an ASCII-armored stream output as a result. The encryption is
done with the public key of the recipient, so the secret passphrase is not required.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to encrypt</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>IDEA</p></li><li><p>TRIPLE_DES</p></li><li><p>CAST5</p></li><li><p>BLOWFISH</p></li><li><p>SAFER</p></li><li><p>DES</p></li><li><p>AES_128</p></li><li><p>AES_192</p></li><li><p>AES_256</p></li><li><p>TWOFISH</p></li><li><p>CAMELLIA_128</p></li><li><p>CAMELLIA_192</p></li><li><p>CAMELLIA_256</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>the symmetric algorithm to use for encryption</p></td><td><p>AES_256</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>the internal file name to use in the resulting PGP header</p></td><td><p>stream</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Disable MDC</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If set, MDC (modification detection code) will not be used during encryption</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The key ID, as defined in the JCE configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pgp Key Info</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#PgpAsymmetricKeyInfo">Pgp Asymmetric Key Info</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>An inline key definition.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that storesoperationâs output will be placed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Pgp](#Pgp)

### Throws

- CRYPTO:ENCRYPTION
- CRYPTO:KEY
- CRYPTO:MISSING_KEY

## Pgp Encrypt And Sign

`<crypto:pgp-encrypt-and-sign>`

Encrypt a stream using PGP, giving an ASCII-armored stream output as a result. The encryption is
done with the public key of the recipient, so the secret passphrase is not required.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to encrypt</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>IDEA</p></li><li><p>TRIPLE_DES</p></li><li><p>CAST5</p></li><li><p>BLOWFISH</p></li><li><p>SAFER</p></li><li><p>DES</p></li><li><p>AES_128</p></li><li><p>AES_192</p></li><li><p>AES_256</p></li><li><p>TWOFISH</p></li><li><p>CAMELLIA_128</p></li><li><p>CAMELLIA_192</p></li><li><p>CAMELLIA_256</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>the symmetric algorithm to use for encryption</p></td><td><p>AES_256</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>the internal file name to use in the resulting PGP header</p></td><td><p>stream</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Encryption Key Selection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#PgpKeySelection">Pgp Key Selection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>the key used to encrypt the message.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Sign Key Selection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#PgpKeySelection">Pgp Key Selection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>the key used to sign the message.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Disable MDC</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If set, MDC (modification detection code) will not be used during encryption</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that storesoperationâs output will be placed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Pgp](#Pgp)

### Throws

- CRYPTO:ENCRYPTION
- CRYPTO:KEY
- CRYPTO:MISSING_KEY

## Pgp Encrypt Binary

`<crypto:pgp-encrypt-binary>`

Encrypt a stream using PGP, giving a binary output as a result. The encryption is done with the
public key of the recipient, so the secret passphrase is not required.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to encrypt</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>IDEA</p></li><li><p>TRIPLE_DES</p></li><li><p>CAST5</p></li><li><p>BLOWFISH</p></li><li><p>SAFER</p></li><li><p>DES</p></li><li><p>AES_128</p></li><li><p>AES_192</p></li><li><p>AES_256</p></li><li><p>TWOFISH</p></li><li><p>CAMELLIA_128</p></li><li><p>CAMELLIA_192</p></li><li><p>CAMELLIA_256</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>the symmetric algorithm to use for encryption</p></td><td><p>AES_256</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>the internal file name to use in the resulting PGP header</p></td><td><p>stream</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Disable MDC</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If set, MDC (modification detection code) will not be used during encryption</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The key ID, as defined in the JCE configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pgp Key Info</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#PgpAsymmetricKeyInfo">Pgp Asymmetric Key Info</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>An inline key definition.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that storesoperationâs output will be placed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Pgp](#Pgp)

### Throws

- CRYPTO:ENCRYPTION
- CRYPTO:KEY
- CRYPTO:MISSING_KEY

## Pgp Sign

`<crypto:pgp-sign>`

Create a detached (standalone) PGP signature of the stream. The signing is done with the private key
of the sender, so the secret passphrase must be provided.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to sign</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>MD5</p></li><li><p>RIPEMD160</p></li><li><p>TIGER_192</p></li><li><p>HAVAL_5_160</p></li><li><p>DOUBLE_SHA</p></li><li><p>SHA1</p></li><li><p>SHA224</p></li><li><p>SHA256</p></li><li><p>SHA384</p></li><li><p>SHA512</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>the digest (or hashing) algorithm</p></td><td><p>SHA256</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The key ID, as defined in the JCE configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pgp Key Info</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#PgpAsymmetricKeyInfo">Pgp Asymmetric Key Info</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>An inline key definition.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that storesoperationâs output will be placed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Pgp](#Pgp)

### Throws

- CRYPTO:KEY
- CRYPTO:MISSING_KEY
- CRYPTO:PARAMETERS
- CRYPTO:PASSPHRASE
- CRYPTO:SIGNATURE

## Pgp Sign Binary

`<crypto:pgp-sign-binary>`

Create a detached (standalone) PGP signature of the stream. The signing is done with the private key
of the sender, so the secret passphrase must be provided.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to sign</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>MD5</p></li><li><p>RIPEMD160</p></li><li><p>TIGER_192</p></li><li><p>HAVAL_5_160</p></li><li><p>DOUBLE_SHA</p></li><li><p>SHA1</p></li><li><p>SHA224</p></li><li><p>SHA256</p></li><li><p>SHA384</p></li><li><p>SHA512</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>the digest (or hashing) algorithm</p></td><td><p>SHA256</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The key ID, as defined in the JCE configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pgp Key Info</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#PgpAsymmetricKeyInfo">Pgp Asymmetric Key Info</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>An inline key definition.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that storesoperationâs output will be placed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Pgp](#Pgp)

### Throws

- CRYPTO:KEY
- CRYPTO:MISSING_KEY
- CRYPTO:PARAMETERS
- CRYPTO:PASSPHRASE
- CRYPTO:SIGNATURE

## Pgp Validate

`<crypto:pgp-validate>`

Validate a PGP signature against a stream, to authenticate it. The validation is done with the
public key of the sender, so the secret passphrase is not required.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the message to authenticate</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expected</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the signature</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Pgp](#Pgp)

### Throws

- CRYPTO:MISSING_KEY
- CRYPTO:PARAMETERS
- CRYPTO:VALIDATION

## Calculate Checksum

`<crypto:calculate-checksum>`

Calculate the checksum of the stream.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>CRC32</p></li><li><p>MD2</p></li><li><p>MD5</p></li><li><p>SHA_1</p></li><li><p>SHA_256</p></li><li><p>SHA_512</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>the checksum algorithm</p></td><td><p>SHA_512</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content for calculating the checksum</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that storesoperationâs output will be placed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Throws

- CRYPTO:CHECKSUM

## Jce Decrypt Pbe

`<crypto:jce-decrypt-pbe>`

Decrypt a stream using JCE, with a password.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to decrypt</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>the algorithm for generating a key from the password</p></td><td><p>PBKDF2withHmacSHA512<em>AES256</em>CBC__PKCS5Padding (FIPS)</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>the password for decryption</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password Salt</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password salt used for encryption or decryption. Recommended minimum: 16 bytes of random data to ensure uniqueness and prevent rainbow table attacks. The salt is encoded in UTF-8 and cannot be modified after creation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Iteration Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>the iteration count used to sign. Recommended minimum: 100,000 iterations to slow down brute-force attacks in modern hardware.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that storesoperationâs output will be placed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Throws

- CRYPTO:DECRYPTION
- CRYPTO:ENCRYPTION
- CRYPTO:KEY
- CRYPTO:MISSING_KEY
- CRYPTO:PARAMETERS
- CRYPTO:PASSPHRASE

## Jce Encrypt Pbe

`<crypto:jce-encrypt-pbe>`

Encrypt a stream using JCE, with a password.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to encrypt</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>the algorithm for generating a key from the password</p></td><td><p>PBKDF2withHmacSHA512<em>AES256</em>CBC__PKCS5Padding</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>the password for encryption</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password Salt</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password salt used for encryption or decryption. Recommended minimum: 16 bytes of random data to ensure uniqueness and prevent rainbow table attacks. The salt is encoded in UTF-8 and cannot be modified after creation.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Iteration Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>the iteration count used to sign. Recommended minimum: 100,000 iterations to slow down brute-force attacks in modern hardware.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that storesoperationâs output will be placed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Throws

- CRYPTO:ENCRYPTION
- CRYPTO:KEY
- CRYPTO:MISSING_KEY
- CRYPTO:PARAMETERS

## Jce Sign Pbe

`<crypto:jce-sign-pbe>`

Sign a stream using JCE, with a password.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to sign</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>the algorithm used for signing</p></td><td><p>PBKDF2withHmacSHA512</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>the password used to sign</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password Salt</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password salt used for encryption or decryption. Recommended minimum: 16 bytes of random data to ensure uniqueness and prevent rainbow table attacks. The salt is encoded in UTF-8 and cannot be modified after creation.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Iteration Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>the iteration count used to sign. Recommended minimum: 100,000 iterations to slow down brute-force attacks in modern hardware.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that storesoperationâs output will be placed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Throws

- CRYPTO:KEY
- CRYPTO:MISSING_KEY
- CRYPTO:PARAMETERS
- CRYPTO:PASSPHRASE
- CRYPTO:SIGNATURE

## Jce Validate Pbe

`<crypto:jce-validate-pbe>`

Validate a stream against a signature, using a password.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>the message to authenticate</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expected</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>the signature to validate</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>the algorithm used for signing</p></td><td><p>PBKDF2withHmacSHA512</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>the password used to sign</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password Salt</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password salt used for encryption or decryption. Recommended minimum: 16 bytes of random data to ensure uniqueness and prevent rainbow table attacks. The salt is encoded in UTF-8 and cannot be modified after creation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Iteration Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>the iteration count used to sign.
